# Finite Elements {#sec-fem}

The FEM layer provides structured finite-element spaces, elements, quadrature, interpolation, vector storage, assembly operations, and FEM-based solvers.

```{mermaid}
classDiagram
  class FiniteElementSpace {
    +numElements()
    +numGlobalDOFs()
    +getLocalDOFIndices()
    +getGlobalDOFIndices()
  }
  class LagrangeSpace {
    +evaluateAx()
    +evaluateLoadVector()
    +evaluateLumpedMass()
    +computeErrorL2()
  }
  class NedelecSpace {
    +evaluateAx()
    +evaluateLoadVector()
    +createFEMVector()
    +reconstructToPoints()
  }
  class FEMVector
  class Element
  class Quadrature
  FiniteElementSpace <|-- LagrangeSpace
  FiniteElementSpace <|-- NedelecSpace
  FiniteElementSpace --> Element
  FiniteElementSpace --> Quadrature
  LagrangeSpace --> FEMVector
  NedelecSpace --> FEMVector
```

## Main classes

| Class | Role |
|---|---|
| `FiniteElementSpace` | Common finite-element space base for structured rectilinear meshes, element indexing, and DOF mapping. |
| `LagrangeSpace` | Nodal Lagrange finite-element space for scalar fields. |
| `NedelecSpace` | Edge-oriented Nedelec finite-element space for vector fields and curl-curl operators. |
| `FEMVector` | FEM vector storage and conversion layer. |
| `FEMInterpolate` | Interpolation helper functionality. |
| `QuadrilateralElement`, `HexahedralElement`, `EdgeElement` | Element definitions. |

## Space construction

A finite-element calculation starts from the same distributed mesh concepts used by fields: a mesh, a domain, and a layout. The FEM space adds an element, a reference element, and a quadrature rule. The space is then responsible for mapping mesh vertices to elements, local DOFs to global DOFs, and element-local data to device kernels.

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  Mesh["Uniform Cartesian mesh"] --> Layout["FieldLayout"]
  Layout --> Space["FiniteElementSpace"]
  Element["Element type"] --> Space
  Quad["Quadrature rule"] --> Space
  Space --> Assembly["evaluateAx / load vector"]
  Space --> Error["error and reconstruction"]
```

The common base provides the indexing vocabulary:

| Method family | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `numElements`, `getElementNDIndex` | Element counts and element-domain indexing. |
| `getMeshVertexNDIndex`, `getElementMeshVertexIndices` | Relationship between mesh vertices and element vertices. |
| `numGlobalDOFs`, `getLocalDOFIndex`, `getGlobalDOFIndex` | Scalar local/global DOF queries. |
| `getLocalDOFIndices`, `getGlobalDOFIndices` | Element-wise DOF index arrays used in assembly. |

## Lagrange spaces

`LagrangeSpace` represents structured-grid nodal Lagrange elements. The number of element DOFs is computed as `(Order + 1)^Dim`. The class exposes the pieces a user expects for elliptic and diffusion-like problems:

| Capability | Use |
|---|---|
| Basis and gradient evaluation | Evaluate shape functions and derivatives at quadrature points. |
| `evaluateAx` | Apply an assembled operator action without exposing the full matrix. |
| `evaluateLoadVector` | Assemble a right-hand-side vector from a source function. |
| `evaluateLumpedMass` | Build lumped mass data for explicit or preconditioned workflows. |
| `computeErrorL2`, `computeAvg` | Measure convergence against analytic fields or averages. |
| `updateLayout` | Rebuild layout-dependent data after distribution changes. |

## Nedelec spaces

`NedelecSpace` provides vector-valued edge DOFs for Maxwell-type curl-curl operators. The current implementation computes the number of element DOFs as `Dim * 2^(Dim - 1)`.

| Capability | Use |
|---|---|
| Shape and curl evaluation | Evaluate vector basis functions and their curls. |
| `getFEMVectorDOFIndices` | Map FEM vector entries to local element DOFs. |
| `evaluateAx` | Apply curl-curl plus mass-like operators. |
| `evaluateLoadVector`, `evaluateLoadVectorFunctor` | Build source vectors from functions or functors. |
| `createFEMVector` | Construct storage compatible with the distributed FEM space. |
| `reconstructToPoints` | Reconstruct vector fields at physical points. |
| Boundary DOF queries | Identify DOFs affected by boundary conditions. |

## Tests to document first

| Test family | Manual purpose |
|---|---|
| `unit_tests/FEM/FiniteElementSpace.cpp` | Validate element indexing and local/global DOF mapping. |
| `unit_tests/FEM/LagrangeSpace.cpp` | Validate scalar basis functions, assembly, and reconstruction. |
| `unit_tests/FEM/NedelecSpace.cpp` | Validate edge basis functions, curls, and vector DOF mapping. |
| `test/solver/fem/TestZeroBC_sin*` | Show homogeneous Dirichlet Poisson-style verification. |
| `test/solver/fem/TestPeriodicBC_sinsin*` | Show periodic finite-element verification. |
| `test/solver/fem/TestNonhomDirichlet_*` | Show non-homogeneous boundary treatment. |
| `test/solver/fem/TestMaxwellDiffusion*` | Show Nedelec-based Maxwell diffusion verification. |

## Developer focus

FEM documentation should be unusually explicit about local-to-global degree-of-freedom mapping, boundary handling, assembly contracts, and which code runs in device contexts.
